title: deployment.export.failed
description: Outbound failure event emitted by anvilkit-export-worker after the CAS transition to EXPORT_FAILED (CAS-then-emit, FR-013). Minimum schema per PRD 0010 §10.3.2. errorCode values preserve the PRD 0010 §13 registry exactly (PRD 0008 §12.2 plus the four per-service auth codes); adding a code requires a contracts version note. failedStage reuses the trace-span vocabulary of PRD 0010 §15.3. attempt follows the §10.3.1 counting rule: maxRetries = 3 means attempt values 0..3 (one initial + three retry executions). Emission is at-least-once — consumers must be duplicate-tolerant keyed by deploymentId. Additive evolution only — removing or renaming a field requires a schema version bump (FR-002).

deploymentId.description: Deployment record ID — consumer dedup key

version.description: Published version the job was exporting

renderMode.description: Render mode of the failed job

errorCode.description: Final classified error code — full PRD 0010 §13 registry. STATUS_CONFLICT and CONFIG_MISSING are listed for registry completeness but do not normally appear in emitted events (409 stops the worker safely without an EXPORT_FAILED transition; CONFIG_MISSING is a boot-time failure before any job starts).

errorClassification.description: Retry classification of errorCode per PRD 0010 §13

failedStage.description: Pipeline stage that failed — trace-span vocabulary of PRD 0010 §15.3

attempt.description: Business retry attempt at failure time, 0..3 (PRD 0010 §10.3.1 counting rule; pending-message reclaim never increments this)

retryExhausted.description: true when a retryable error exhausted maxRetries = 3 (attempt 3) and the job was terminally failed + DLQ-routed

traceId.description: Trace ID of the producing job (cross-boundary traceability, FR-020)

createdAt.description: Emit time, ISO 8601 — field name matches the inbound event vocabulary (PRD 0008 §8.1)
